diagrams.title: Chomsky Hierarchy Pyramid
diagrams.description: Shows the four types of grammars in the Chomsky hierarchy with their power relationship
elements: Each level includes all languages below it
diagrams.purpose: Visualize that unrestricted grammars generate the largest class of languages, while regular grammars generate the smallest, most restrictive class

diagrams.title: Finite Automaton Diagram
diagrams.description: A simple finite automaton that accepts strings ending with 'ab' over alphabet {a,b}
elements: Circle q2 (double circle): Accept state after 'ab'
Arrow from q0 to q1 labeled 'a'
Arrow from q0 to q0 labeled 'b'
Arrow from q1 to q1 labeled 'b'
Arrow from q1 to q2 labeled 'a'
Arrow from q2 to q2 labeled 'a,b'
diagrams.purpose: Understand how finite automata process input strings and determine acceptance/rejection

diagrams.title: Parse Tree for Expression
elements: Level 2: T → F, F → ( E ) | number; F → number
Shows hierarchical derivation from grammar rules
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how a parser builds tree structure from input string according to grammar rules
